provenance.source_corpus: Roadside revegetation with native plants: Experimental seeding and transplanting of stem cuttings

source_spans.text: The stratification treatment significantly enhanced seed germination of K. angustifolia , I. versicolor , J. effusus and E. vaginatum (p < 0.05)
The stratification treatment significantly enhanced seed germination of K. angustifolia , I. versicolor , J. effusus and E. vaginatum (p < 0.05)
Iris versicolor cover increased significantly in organic matter mulch compared to hay-mat mulch and control.
Iris versicolor cover increased significantly in organic matter mulch compared to hay-mat mulch and control.
growth was reduced in organic matter mulch.
Survival and growth of stem cuttings of E. nigrum and J. communis were significantly increased on hay-mat mulch.
Survival and growth of stem cuttings of E. nigrum and J. communis were significantly increased on hay-mat mulch.
Germination of I. versicolor was lower (5%) in hay-mat mulch than control
Mulching had no significant effect on the survival of transplanted I. versicolor seedlings
Mulching had no significant effect on the survival of transplanted I. versicolor seedlings
Shoot growth of I. versicolor was lower (p < 0.001; F = 30.5) in the mulching treatments than control
Total biomass of I. versicolor was lower (p < 0.001; F = 36.2) in hay-mat mulch and organic matter mulch (by 30 and 70%) than control
However, it caused significant reduction in shoot dry mass compared to organic matter mulch and control.
for J. communis the hay-mat mulch provided significantly (p < 0.001; F = 12.5) more survival (44%) than control and organic matter mulch (25%).
